Bash(cd /Users/mridang/Code/zitadel/example-expressjs-auth && git add src/app.ts test/app.test.ts && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: clear authjs session cookies on logout callback

The logout callback relied solely on the Clear-Site-Data response header to clear Auth.js session cookies, but browsers ignore Clear-Site-Data on 302 redirect responses. This left authjs.session-token \(and friends\) in the browser after logout, so the user remained signed in. The callback now also emits explicit Set-Cookie deletions for every authjs.* cookie and for logout_state \(matching the path it was set on\), which browsers do honor on redirects.
EOF
\)")

Bash(cd /Users/mridang/Code/zitadel/example-fastify-auth && git add src/app.ts test/app.test.ts && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: clear authjs session cookies on logout callback

The logout callback relied solely on the Clear-Site-Data response header to clear Auth.js session cookies, but browsers ignore Clear-Site-Data on 302 redirect responses. This left authjs.session-token \(and friends\) in the browser after logout, so the user remained signed in. The callback now also emits explicit Set-Cookie deletions for every authjs.* cookie and for logout_state \(matching the path it was set on\), which browsers do honor on redirects.
EOF
\)")

Bash(cd /Users/mridang/Code/zitadel/example-hono-auth && git add src/app.ts test/app.test.ts && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'

Bash(gh pr view 20 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit --json state,title,author,url 2>&1)
Bash(gh issue view 18 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit --json state,title,author,url 2>&1)

Bash(gh issue comment 18 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit --body "Fixed in 9197e88. The root cause was twofold:

1. The logout callback relied on the \\`Clear-Site-Data\\` response header to clear cookies, but browsers ignore that header on 302 redirect responses. The callback now also emits explicit \\`Set-Cookie\\` deletions \(\\`Max-Age=0\\`\) for every \\`authjs.*\\` cookie and for \\`logout_state\\`, which browsers do honor on redirects.

2. The Auth.js handle hook was running after the page load and re-setting \\`authjs.callback-url\\` via the \\`redirect\\` callback, overwriting our delete. \\`hooks.server.ts\\` now bypasses \\`authHandle\\` for \\`/auth/logout/callback\\` and \\`+layout.server.ts\\` skips \\`event.locals.auth\(\)\\` for that path.

A Playwright test was added to guard against regressions.")
Bash(gh issue close 18 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit 2>&1)

Bash(gh pr comment 20 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit --body "Thanks for the report and the fix attempt! The underlying bug is fixed in 9197e88 directly on main. Closing this PR in favor of the landed fix.

The fix needed two parts: \(1\) explicit \\`Set-Cookie\\` deletions because browsers ignore \\`Clear-Site-Data\\` on 302 redirects, and \(2\) bypassing the Auth.js handle hook for the callback path because the \\`redirect\\` callback was re-setting \\`authjs.callback-url\\` after the page load deleted it. See #18 for details.")
Bash(gh pr close 20 --repo zitadel/example-auth-sveltekit 2>&1)

Bash(cd /Users/mridang/Code/zitadel/example-nextjs-auth && git add src/app/api/auth/logout/callback/route.ts test/app.spec.ts && git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: clear authjs session cookies on logout callback

The logout callback deleted logout_state without specifying a path, defaulting to / and never matching the cookie which is set with path /api/auth/logout/callback, so the browser kept it. The delete now passes the matching path. The redirect now also explicitly returns 302 instead of the Next.js default 307 to align with the other example repos, and a Playwright test was added to guard against regressions.
EOF
\)" && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -10)
